pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'Save the result to `/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/curved_colonnade.FCStd`'. Confirmed by setup_task.sh (line 13) which creates this directory, and export_result.sh (line 9) which expects the file at this location.

Task description states 'Configure the array with a **Count of at least 5**'. FreeCAD official documentation confirms DataCount is an integer property specifying 'the number of elements in the array'. Verifier.py (lines 115-116) checks if count_val >= min_count. Source: https://wiki.freecadweb.org/Draft_PathArray

Task description states 'ensure **Align is set to True**'. FreeCAD official documentation confirms DataAlign is a Bool property that 'specifies if the elements in the array are aligned along the path or not'. Verifier.py (lines 135-139) checks for the Align property and verifies it equals 'true'. Source: https://wiki.freecadweb.org/Draft_PathArray

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a Path Array in FreeCAD with a minimum count of 5 elements. FreeCAD's Draft PathArray feature supports the DataCount integer property to control the number of array elements, and the DataAlign boolean property to align elements along the path. The output file is expected at /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/curved_colonnade.FCStd, a standard FreeCAD document location. All metadata claims are consistent with official FreeCAD documentation and verified by the task description, setup scripts, and verification logic.
